FSR-009.title: Current Sensor Failure Detection (SG-09, ASIL B)

IT-010.title: Cell voltage flows from AFE to SOA

IT-011.title: Current flows from IVT to SOA

IT-012.title: Temperature flows from AFE to SOA

IT-013.title: State request flows from CAN to BMS

IT-014.title: BMS state flows to CAN TX

IT-039.title: Current on open string triggers ERROR

IT-040.title: Overvoltage below threshold count does not trigger ERROR

IT-041.title: Temperature below threshold count does not trigger ERROR

QT-009.title: Hardware fault emulation via stub injection

SSR-002.title: The software shall detect cell undervoltage below 1500 mV within the FTTI of 10.1 seconds.

SSR-003.title: The software shall detect cell discharge overcurrent above 180000 mA within the FTTI of 1.1 seconds.

SSR-004.title: The software shall detect cell charge overcurrent above 180000 mA within the FTTI of 1.1 seconds.

SSR-005.title: The software shall detect overtemperature discharge above 55 deg C within the FTTI of 500.1 seconds.

SSR-006.title: The software shall detect undertemperature discharge below -20 deg C within the FTTI of 500.1 seconds.

SSR-007.title: The software shall detect overtemperature charge above 45 deg C within the FTTI of 500.1 seconds.

SSR-008.title: The software shall detect undertemperature charge below -20 deg C within the FTTI of 500.1 seconds.

SSR-009.title: The software shall detect deep discharge (cell voltage <= 1500 mV) within 0.21 seconds and latch the fault.

SSR-010.title: The software shall detect current on an open string within 1.1 seconds.

SSR-020.title: Upon detection of any MSL violation (FATAL), the software shall transition the BMS to ERROR state.

SSR-021.title: In ERROR state, the software shall command all three contactors (string+, string-, precharge) to open within one BMS tas

SSR-022.title: The software shall not permit the BMS to exit ERROR state unless the originating fault condition has cleared.

SSR-023.title: The software shall not permit the BMS to exit ERROR state unless an explicit STANDBY request has been received via CAN.

SSR-024.title: SSR-022 and SSR-023 must both be satisfied simultaneously for ERROR exit (AND condition).

SSR-030.title: The DIAG module shall provide debounced fault detection with configurable threshold counters per diagnostic ID.

SSR-031.title: The DIAG module shall prevent false FATAL triggers from transient measurement noise through threshold counting.

SSR-032.title: The DIAG module shall detect persistent faults by incrementing the counter on each evaluation cycle until the threshold

SSR-033.title: The DIAG module shall allow fault recovery by decrementing the counter when the fault condition clears.

SSR-040.title: The software shall detect loss of CAN communication within the configured FTTI (100 events x 200 ms = 20 seconds).

SSR-041.title: The software shall detect loss of current sensor communication within the configured FTTI (100 events x 200 ms = 20 seco

SSR-042.title: The software shall detect AFE communication failure within the configured FTTI (5 events x 100 ms = 0.5 seconds).

SSR-050.title: The software shall monitor contactor feedback and detect mismatches within the configured FTTI (20 events x 100 ms = 2 s

SSR-051.title: The software shall not close contactors unless the BMS is in a state that permits contactor closure (PRECHARGE or NORMAL

SSR-052.title: The software shall respect the contactor maximum break current limit of 3500 mA during normal disconnect sequences.

SW-REQ-040.title: The BMS state machine shall implement the states: STANDBY (5), PRECHARGE (6), NORMAL (7), ERROR (9).

SW-REQ-041.title: Transition from STANDBY to PRECHARGE shall occur only upon receipt of a valid state request via CAN ID 0x210.

SW-REQ-042.title: Transition from PRECHARGE to NORMAL shall occur when precharge conditions are met (voltage within tolerance).

SW-REQ-043.title: Transition from any operational state to ERROR shall occur when any FATAL diagnostic flag is set.

SW-REQ-044.title: In ERROR state, the BMS shall command all three contactors (string+, string-, precharge) to open.

SW-REQ-050.title: The SYS state machine shall implement states: UNINITIALIZED (0), INITIALIZATION (1), INITIALIZED (2), IDLE (3), RUNNING

SW-REQ-053.title: SYS shall transition from IDLE to RUNNING when all subsystems report ready.

SYS-REQ-001.title: The BMS shall support the configured number of battery strings.

SYS-REQ-002.title: Each string shall contain the configured number of modules.

SYS-REQ-003.title: Each module shall contain the configured number of cells in series.

SYS-REQ-004.title: Each cell block shall contain the configured number of parallel cells.

SYS-REQ-005.title: Each module shall support the configured number of temperature sensors.

SYS-REQ-006.title: The system shall manage the configured number of contactors.

SYS-REQ-007.title: The system shall accept HV voltage inputs from current sensor.

SYS-REQ-010.title: The BMS shall record cell nominal capacity.

SYS-REQ-011.title: The BMS shall record cell energy rating.

SYS-REQ-012.title: The BMS shall record cell nominal voltage.

SYS-REQ-013.title: The BMS shall enforce maximum string current limit.

SYS-REQ-014.title: The BMS shall enforce maximum pack current limit.

SYS-REQ-015.title: Contactor maximum break current shall not be exceeded.

SYS-REQ-016.title: The BMS shall detect rest current condition.

SYS-REQ-050.title: The BMS shall maintain a diagnostic module with at least 85 diagnostic identifiers.

SYS-REQ-051.title: Each diagnostic identifier shall have a configurable threshold counter (number of events before triggering).

SYS-REQ-052.title: Each diagnostic identifier shall have a configurable evaluation delay (period between consecutive checks).

SYS-REQ-053.title: A diagnostic reaching FATAL severity shall cause the BMS to transition to ERROR state within the configured FTTI.

SYS-REQ-054.title: In ERROR state the BMS shall open all three contactors (string+, string-, precharge).

SYS-REQ-055.title: The BMS shall not exit ERROR state until the fault is cleared AND an explicit STANDBY request (0x210 byte 0 = 0x00) is r

SYS-REQ-056.title: Voltage fault FTTI shall be <= 700 ms (threshold=50 events x 10 ms period = 500 ms detection + 200 ms delay).

SYS-REQ-057.title: Current fault FTTI shall be <= 200 ms (threshold=10 x 10 ms = 100 ms detection + 100 ms delay).

SYS-REQ-058.title: Temperature fault FTTI shall be <= 6000 ms (threshold=500 x 10 ms = 5000 ms detection + 1000 ms delay).

SYS-REQ-059.title: Contactor feedback fault FTTI shall be <= 300 ms (threshold=20 x 10 ms = 200 ms + 100 ms delay).

SYS-REQ-060.title: The BMS shall transmit the BMS state message.

SYS-REQ-061.title: The BMS shall transmit the BMS state details message.

SYS-REQ-062.title: The BMS shall transmit cell voltage summary.

SYS-REQ-063.title: The BMS shall transmit cell temperature summary.

SYS-REQ-064.title: The BMS shall transmit pack values P0 (voltage and current).

SYS-REQ-065.title: The BMS shall transmit pack values P1 (power).

SYS-REQ-067.title: The BMS shall transmit multiplexed cell voltages.

SYS-REQ-068.title: The BMS shall transmit multiplexed cell temperatures.

SYS-REQ-069.title: The BMS shall transmit slave info.

SYS-REQ-070.title: The BMS shall accept state request messages.

SYS-REQ-071.title: The BMS shall accept AFE cell voltage messages.

SYS-REQ-072.title: The BMS shall accept AFE cell temperature messages.

SYS-REQ-073.title: The BMS shall accept IVT current measurement.

SYS-REQ-074.title: The BMS shall accept IVT voltage 1 measurement.

SYS-REQ-075.title: The BMS shall accept IVT voltage 2 measurement.

SYS-REQ-076.title: The BMS shall accept IVT voltage 3 (HV bus voltage) for precharge and redundancy checks.

SYS-REQ-077.title: The BMS shall accept IVT temperature measurement.

SYS-REQ-080.title: SYS shall transition from UNINITIALIZED to INITIALIZATION on startup.

SYS-REQ-081.title: SYS shall transition from INIT to INITIALIZED when all init conditions are met.

SYS-REQ-082.title: SYS shall transition from INITIALIZED to IDLE automatically.

SYS-REQ-083.title: SYS shall transition from IDLE to RUNNING when all subsystems are ready.

SYS-REQ-084.title: SYS shall remain in RUNNING for the entire operational lifetime unless a system-level fault occurs.

SYS-REQ-090.title: BMS shall not activate until SYS state == RUNNING.

SYS-REQ-091.title: BMS shall transition from STANDBY to PRECHARGE on NORMAL request.

SYS-REQ-092.title: BMS shall transition from PRECHARGE to NORMAL when precharge voltage converges.

SYS-REQ-093.title: BMS shall transition from any operational state to ERROR on FATAL diagnostic.

SYS-REQ-094.title: BMS shall transition from ERROR to STANDBY only when fault cleared AND STANDBY requested.

SYS-REQ-095.title: BMS shall abort precharge on timeout.

SYS-REQ-100.title: On PRECHARGE entry, the BMS shall close the precharge contactor (SPS ch2) and string- contactor (SPS ch1).

SYS-REQ-101.title: The BMS shall compute V_string as the sum of all 18 cell voltages received from the AFE (CAN 0x270).

SYS-REQ-102.title: The BMS shall use IVT Voltage 3 (CAN 0x524, stored as highVoltage_mV[s][2]) as V_bus for the precharge comparison.

SYS-REQ-104.title: On successful precharge (voltage convergence), the BMS shall close string+ contactor (SPS ch0) and then open precharge c

SYS-REQ-105.title: On precharge timeout (voltage does not converge within configured time), the BMS shall abort by opening all contactors a

SYS-REQ-110.title: The BMS shall control three contactors via the SPS driver: ch0 = string+ contactor, ch1 = string- contactor, ch2 = prech

SYS-REQ-111.title: During STANDBY, all three SPS channels shall be OFF (all contactors open).

SYS-REQ-112.title: During PRECHARGE, SPS ch1 (string-) and ch2 (precharge) shall be ON; ch0 (string+) shall be OFF.

SYS-REQ-113.title: During NORMAL, SPS ch0 (string+) and ch1 (string-) shall be ON; ch2 (precharge) shall be OFF.

SYS-REQ-114.title: On ERROR entry, the BMS shall open all three contactors within one control cycle (10 ms).

SYS-REQ-115.title: The SPS driver shall verify contactor feedback (actual state matches requested state) within 20 evaluation events.

SYS-REQ-120.title: The BMS shall use voltage-based balancing: any cell where V_cell > V_min + BAL_threshold shall be balanced (discharged).

SYS-REQ-121.title: Balancing shall employ hysteresis: stop balancing a cell when V_cell <= V_min + BAL_threshold + hysteresis.

SYS-REQ-122.title: Balancing shall only be active when BMS state == NORMAL.

SYS-REQ-123.title: Balancing shall only be active when \

SYS-REQ-130.title: The BMS shall estimate SOC using Coulomb counting: SOC(t) = SOC(t-1) - (I x dt) / (Capacity x 3600).

SYS-REQ-131.title: The initial SOC at startup shall be 50%.

SYS-REQ-132.title: The BMS shall report SOC on CAN message 0x235 byte 5, encoded as SOC percentage x 2 (e.g., 50% = 0x64, 100% = 0xC8).

SYS-REQ-133.title: SOC shall be clamped to the range 0-100%.

SYS-REQ-134.title: SOC shall be updated and transmitted every 100 ms.

SYS-REQ-140.title: The plant model shall send all CAN RX messages (0x210, 0x270, 0x280, 0x521-0x524, 0x527) at 100 ms period on SocketCAN.

SYS-REQ-141.title: The plant model shall simulate 18 cell voltages derived from OCV(SOC) curve in the range 3400-4200 mV, with IR drop of 5

SYS-REQ-142.title: The plant model shall encode cell voltages into CAN 0x270 using 50 mux groups x 4 voltages, 13-bit unsigned big-endian,

SYS-REQ-143.title: The plant model shall simulate IVT current: 0 A in STANDBY, 10 A discharge in NORMAL (closed-loop from contactor state).

SYS-REQ-144.title: The plant model shall simulate IVT voltages: V_pack = 18 x V_cell - I x R_internal.

SYS-REQ-145.title: The plant model shall simulate cell temperatures at 25 deg C (static).

SYS-REQ-146.title: The plant model shall send state request: STANDBY (0x00) for the first 3 s, then NORMAL (0x02).

SYS-REQ-147.title: The plant model IVT Voltage 3 (0x524) shall reflect the HV bus voltage for precharge verification: 0 V when contactors o

SYS-REQ-150.title: The POSIX port shall compile foxBMS 2 v1.10.0 for x86-64 using GCC 13.

SYS-REQ-151.title: The POSIX port shall replace FreeRTOS with a cooperative main loop.

SYS-REQ-152.title: The POSIX port shall replace hardware CAN with SocketCAN.

SYS-REQ-153.title: The POSIX port shall suppress 24 hardware-absent diagnostic IDs.

SYS-REQ-154.title: The POSIX port shall retain 61 software-checkable diagnostic IDs.

SYS-REQ-155.title: The POSIX port shall provide at least 80 HAL stub modules.

SYS-REQ-156.title: The POSIX port shall map 60+ TMS570 register bases to RAM buffers.

UT-002.title: DIAG_Handler decrements counter on OK event

UT-003.title: DIAG_Handler sets FATAL flag when counter reaches threshold

UT-005.title: DIAG_IsAnyFatalErrorSet returns true when any FATAL flag set

UT-006.title: DIAG_IsAnyFatalErrorSet returns false when no FATAL flag set

UT-008.title: All FATAL entries have non-zero thresholds

UT-010.title: SOA detects overvoltage at MOL threshold (2720 mV)

UT-011.title: SOA detects overvoltage at RSL threshold (2750 mV)

UT-012.title: SOA detects overvoltage at MSL threshold (2800 mV)

UT-013.title: SOA detects undervoltage at MSL threshold (1500 mV)

UT-014.title: SOA detects deep discharge at 1500 mV

UT-015.title: SOA reports OK when voltage within normal range

UT-016.title: SOA detects discharge overcurrent at MSL (180000 mA)

UT-017.title: SOA detects charge overcurrent at MSL (180000 mA)

UT-018.title: SOA detects overtemperature discharge at MSL (55 deg C)

UT-019.title: SOA detects undertemperature discharge at MSL (-20 deg C)

UT-020.title: SOA detects overtemperature charge at MSL (45 deg C)

UT-021.title: SOA detects undertemperature charge at MSL (-20 deg C)

UT-031.title: BMS transitions STANDBY to PRECHARGE on valid request

UT-032.title: BMS transitions PRECHARGE to NORMAL on precharge complete

UT-033.title: BMS transitions to ERROR on FATAL diagnostic

UT-034.title: BMS opens all contactors in ERROR state

UT-035.title: BMS remains in ERROR until fault cleared AND STANDBY requested

UT-036.title: BMS transitions ERROR to STANDBY when both conditions met

UT-040.title: Database write and read returns same data

UT-041.title: Database read returns most recent write

UT-042.title: Database entries exist for all configured measurements

UT-050.title: CAN transmit formats BMS state message at ID 0x220

UT-051.title: CAN receive decodes state request from ID 0x210

UT-052.title: CAN receive decodes IVT frames from IDs 0x521-0x527

UT-060.title: Contactor set state OPEN commands the physical output

UT-061.title: Contactor set state CLOSE commands the physical output

UT-062.title: Contactor feedback mismatch reported to DIAG

UT-103.title: CAN TX messages appear on SocketCAN

UT-104.title: Binary runs for 10 seconds without error

UT-105.title: No segfault or abort during startup

UT-110.title: All 80+ HAL stub functions are callable

UT-111.title: Register base pointers map to valid RAM
